title: News and updates | Employment New Zealand
content:
## News and updates Ngā rongo kōrero me ngā whakahou
News and media releases from Employment New Zealand.
- 1 Aug 2025 Southland company which exploited workers fined for obstructing ERA A Southland dairy business and its owner who have paid over $116,000 in arrears owed to 3 employees and were last year ordered to pay penalties of $215,000 by the Employment Relations Authority (ERA) for minimum employment standards breaches relating to those arrears, have been penalised a further $15,000 for obstructing the ERA’s investigation. Migrant exploitation
A Southland dairy business and its owner who have paid over $116,000 in arrears owed to 3 employees and were last year ordered to pay penalties of $215,000 by the Employment Relations Authority (ERA) for minimum employment standards breaches relating to those arrears, have been penalised a further $15,000 for obstructing the ERA’s investigation.
- Migrant exploitation
- 17 Jul 2025 Lower Hutt restaurant fined $30,000 for exploiting migrant worker The Employment Relations Authority has ordered a Lower Hutt sushi restaurant to pay $30,000 in penalties after breaching multiple employment standards which negatively affected a migrant worker. Migrant exploitation
The Employment Relations Authority has ordered a Lower Hutt sushi restaurant to pay $30,000 in penalties after breaching multiple employment standards which negatively affected a migrant worker.
- Migrant exploitation
- 1 Jul 2025 Changes to the rules for partial strikes Recent changes to Employment Relations Act 2000 affect how employers can handle pay when employees participate in a partial strike.
Recent changes to Employment Relations Act 2000 affect how employers can handle pay when employees participate in a partial strike.
- 26 Jun 2025 Parental leave payments to increase from 1 July Parental leave payments will increase from 1 July 2025. Parental leave
Parental leave payments will increase from 1 July 2025.
- 26 Jun 2025 Changes to parental leave laws from 1 July Changes to the Parental Leave and Employment Protection Act 1987 take effect from 1 July 2025. Parental leave
Changes to the Parental Leave and Employment Protection Act 1987 take effect from 1 July 2025.
- 14 May 2025 Changes to Equal Pay Act The Government has announced changes to the Equal Pay Act
The Government has announced changes to the Equal Pay Act
- 30 Apr 2025 Law changes about employment agreements Changes to the Employment Relations Act 2000 came into force on 30 March 2025. They introduced rules about having easy access to employment agreements. Employment agreements
Changes to the Employment Relations Act 2000 came into force on 30 March 2025. They introduced rules about having easy access to employment agreements.
- Employment agreements
- 10 Apr 2025 Labour Inspectorate swoops on Bay of Islands Last week the Labour Inspectorate launched an extensive compliance operation in the Bay of Islands. Labour Inspectors visited dozens of businesses.
Last week the Labour Inspectorate launched an extensive compliance operation in the Bay of Islands. Labour Inspectors visited dozens of businesses.
- 17 Mar 2025 Labour Inspectorate in nationwide employer compliance clampdown Scores of employers across the country can expect a visit from Labour Inspectors soon as the Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment’s (MBIE) Labour Inspectorate clamps down on errant business owners. Legal case
Scores of employers across the country can expect a visit from Labour Inspectors soon as the Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment’s (MBIE) Labour Inspectorate clamps down on errant business owners.
- 11 Feb 2025 Retail, hospitality businesses in Labour Inspectorate spotlight The Labour Inspectorate has launched its first operation for 2025, making unannounced visits to 13 retail and hospitality businesses in Hamilton to check compliance with minimum employment standards. Migrant exploitation
The Labour Inspectorate has launched its first operation for 2025, making unannounced visits to 13 retail and hospitality businesses in Hamilton to check compliance with minimum employment standards.
- Migrant exploitation
